title: Bachelor of Science in Health Information Management
content: The Bachelor of Science in Health Information Management programme aims to develop skilled, competent, and ethical Health Information Professionals capable of managing, analyzing, and safeguarding patient health data in healthcare settings. The programme combines academic knowledge and hands-on clinical training to equip graduates with skills in medical coding, electronic health records (EHR), data analytics, quality assurance, and healthcare compliance. Upon completion of the programme, graduates will be proficient in health data management, clinical documentation, coding and classification systems, health information systems, and legal and ethical frameworks for patient data. They will be able to ensure data accuracy, confidentiality, compliance with regulatory standards, and contribute effectively as part of multidisciplinary healthcare teams. Class XII or equivalent examination with a minimum of 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology / Mathematics / Computer Science. The minimum age should be 17 years as on December 31st of each Admission Year. (Medical fitness certificate is mandatory to submit and it is to be endorsed by Government Medical Officer) 01 oundational & Technical Knowledge: Apply knowledge of biomedical sciences, healthcare systems, and information management principles to manage health data effectively. 02 Data Analysis & Decision-Making: Analyze health data for clinical, operational, and administrative decision-making. 03 Coding & Documentation Skills: Perform accurate medical coding, clinical documentation, and reporting using standardized classification systems. 04 Health Information Technology & Systems: Operate, manage, and troubleshoot electronic health records (EHR) and other health information systems. 05 Data Security & Privacy: Implement security measures and ensure confidentiality, integrity, and compliance with legal and ethical standards for patient data. 06 Quality Assurance & Risk Management: Apply quality assurance protocols and risk management strategies to improve healthcare services and patient outcomes. 07 Communication & Teamwork: Communicate effectively with healthcare professionals, administrators, and stakeholders to facilitate proper use and interpretation of health data. 08 Research & Evidence-Based Practice: Apply research and data-driven approaches to improve health information management and contribute to public health initiatives. 09 Professionalism & Lifelong Learning: Demonstrate professionalism, leadership, and continuous learning to adapt to emerging technologies and practices in health information management. 01 Professional Competence: Graduates will demonstrate proficiency in managing healthcare information systems, clinical documentation, and health data analytics. 02 Ethical & Compliant Practice: Graduates will maintain patient confidentiality, adhere to legal and ethical standards, and implement quality assurance measures in health information management. 03 Lifelong Learning & Research: Graduates will engage in continuous professional development, advanced certifications, and research to adopt emerging technologies and best practices. 04 Leadership, Teamwork & Community Contribution: Graduates will demonstrate leadership, teamwork, and effective communication, contributing to healthcare organizations, public health initiatives, and community health programs. Class XII or equivalent examination with a minimum of 50% marks in Physics, Chemistry, and Biology / Mathematics / Computer Science. The minimum age should be 17 years as on December 31st of each Admission Year. (Medical fitness certificate is mandatory to submit and it is to be endorsed by Government Medical Officer)
